verdict_rationale: The two structural smells that drove the prior AUDIT_WEAK verdict (the Liskov outlier on parse_opencode_agent_md, and three sibling adapters importing five private helpers from claude_io) are both closed; remaining findings are WARNING or INFO.
remediation_diff_summary: Phase 2 extracted the YAML-frontmatter prelude into a dedicated yaml_frontmatter module with a public surface; all four Markdown adapters now import from there instead of from claude_io. Phase 3 added field_names.py with CanonicalField/ClaudeField/CodexField/OpencodeField vocabularies and CROSS_ADAPTER_FIELD_MAP, adopted in claude_io only. Phase 4 reshaped parse_opencode_agent_md to raise when no name source is available (closing CQ-01) and version-gated the v0.1 review-marker strip (closing CQ-16).
detailed_analysis: The Liskov outlier in parse_opencode_agent_md is closed: it now raises when none of (artifact_path, prior_canonical.name, frontmatter.name) can produce a non-empty identity; three new tests cover all three branches. yaml_frontmatter exports FRONTMATTER_RE, yaml_load/dump, strip_bom_prefix, split_frontmatter, frontmatter_for_render, extract_pair_id_from_md, AdapterParseError. Every Markdown adapter imports from it. The 14-line parse-prelude exists in exactly one place. The codex TOML path still has its own _normalize_toml_text / _make_yaml / _yaml_dump private helpers and its own copy of _CORRUPTED_UTF8_BOM. The render-side prelude still appears verbatim in four modules. The new field_names.py is adopted only in claude_io; opencode_io.FOREIGN_AGENT_FIELDS / FOREIGN_SKILL_FIELDS still hardcode 'disallowedTools', 'permissionMode', 'mcpServers', 'developer_instructions' as bare strings. CQ-09 mostly closed but one residual `from agents_sync.canonical import SCHEMA_VERSION` lives inside _strip_legacy_review_metadata. CQ-06 partially closed: yaml_frontmatter.AdapterParseError exists but codex_io:278 (TOML root) and opencode_io:197 (name-source check) still raise bare ValueError. CQ-12 persists: no tests/fixtures/ directory.

findings.description: Parse-side prelude is fully centralized in yaml_frontmatter.split_frontmatter. Render-side prelude is still duplicated verbatim in claude_io:146-150, antigravity_io:134-138, opencode_io:96-100 (as _render_markdown helper), and slash_command_io:214-218. Four copies, one wrapped.
findings.recommendation: Promote opencode_io._render_markdown to yaml_frontmatter.render_markdown_with_frontmatter(frontmatter, body) and call it from all four sites.

findings.description: yaml_frontmatter.strip_bom_prefix centralized the markdown side. codex_io._normalize_toml_text (line 67) and the _CORRUPTED_UTF8_BOM constant (line 24) are still local. The TOML and Markdown BOM-handling are byte-equivalent but the constants are declared twice in slightly different escape forms.
findings.recommendation: Have codex_io._normalize_toml_text call yaml_frontmatter.strip_bom_prefix and remove the local _CORRUPTED_UTF8_BOM constant. Same for codex_io._make_yaml / _yaml_dump.

findings.description: `if not isinstance(data, dict): raise ValueError(...)` at codex_io.py line 277 remains unreachable — tomllib.loads always returns dict on success and raises tomllib.TOMLDecodeError on failure.
findings.recommendation: Drop the isinstance check, or replace with `assert isinstance(data, dict)`.

findings.description: yaml_frontmatter.AdapterParseError provides a single exception type for malformed frontmatter on every Markdown adapter. codex_io:278 (TOML root) and opencode_io:197 (name-source check) still raise bare ValueError.
findings.recommendation: Have codex_io and opencode_io raise AdapterParseError for these adapter-local malformed-input cases too.

findings.description: _toml_scalar / _toml_key / _toml_section_name / _emit_toml_table / _toml_dump still implement a hand-rolled TOML emitter that supports only str/bool/int/float/flat-list and raises TypeError on datetime, NaN, Infinity, nested lists, byte strings, None. Supported subset undocumented and not pinned by negative tests.
findings.recommendation: Either document the supported subset in the module docstring with negative-tests, or switch to tomlkit / tomli_w.

findings.description: Phase 2 moved most function-scoped imports to module scope. One residual remains: `from agents_sync.canonical import SCHEMA_VERSION` inside _strip_legacy_review_metadata. canonical does not import codex_io, so no cycle justifies it.
findings.recommendation: Promote the SCHEMA_VERSION import to module scope.

findings.description: FOREIGN_AGENT_FIELDS and FOREIGN_SKILL_FIELDS still hardcode the set of Claude/Codex field names as bare string literals. field_names.py provides exactly these names as ClaudeField.* / CodexField.* constants.
findings.recommendation: Reference ClaudeField.* / CodexField.* constants in the two frozensets instead of raw strings.

findings.description: No parse -> render -> parse -> render fixture-stability test exists for the codex agent path.
findings.recommendation: Add test_codex_agent_parse_then_render_then_parse_is_a_fixed_point with a realistic TOML fixture.

findings.description: No tests/fixtures/ directory was added. Every adapter test still constructs inputs with inline textwrap.dedent strings.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/fixtures/captured/{claude,codex,antigravity,opencode}/ with at least one real .md/.toml each.

findings.description: Adoption of field_names is uneven: claude_io migrated; codex_io.KNOWN_CODEX_TOML_KEYS, antigravity_io.KNOWN_ANTIGRAVITY_FIELDS, and opencode_io.KNOWN_OPENCODE_*_FIELDS still use raw string literals.
findings.recommendation: Migrate the remaining three KNOWN_* frozensets to reference field_names.* constants.

findings.description: claude_io imports FRONTMATTER_RE and extract_pair_id_from_md from yaml_frontmatter. Neither name is referenced in claude_io.py; extract_pair_id_from_md is consumed by one external caller via the legacy path.
findings.recommendation: Drop FRONTMATTER_RE from the claude_io import list; have tool_specs/codex.py import extract_pair_id_from_md from yaml_frontmatter directly.
